channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Tariffs Confound Small Businesses Again
item.description: Chocolate makers and fig-paste importers are facing a tangle of unknowns, including whether to seek refunds for tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Cou...

item.title: Boards aren’t ready for the AI age: What happens when your CEO gets deepfaked?
item.description: Deepfake attacks have moved from cyber briefings to boardroom emergencies with every CEO likeness a potential vulnerability. Most companies have no pl...

item.title: Asia’s founders are spending more money on AI tools, with use of some coding tools rising by more than four times
item.description: Spending on AI tools by founders rose 20% last year, according to Aspire, a Singapore-based fintech firm.

item.title: Goldman Sachs vice chair on the hidden trap of senior management: ‘pretty soon the bosses are no longer watching you’
item.description: Before returning as vice chair, Rob Kaplan served on the faculty of the Harvard Business School and as president of the Dallas Federal Reserve.

item.title: Want to live forever? Meta patented an AI model that would keep your profile active after you die
item.description: Meta’s new AI patent means your dead uncle could still comment fire emojis on your posts, but experts say this could undermine grief and closure.

item.title: JPMorgan’s CEO Jamie Dimon reveals the career goal he adopted when he was just a 28-year-old assistant: Do not speak unless you can add value
item.description: Before Jamie Dimon was the billionaire boss of JPMorgan, he set himself a surprisingly modest rule: don’t talk—it propelled him to a CFO role by 30

item.title: Texas and North Carolina Elections Will Test Older House Democrats’ Staying Power
item.description: Starting in North Carolina on Tuesday, older House Democrats face a test of their staying power and the appetite of voters to replace them.

item.title: Trump’s Foreign Policy Vision: Imperialism
item.description: Donald Trump’s foreign policy vision is imperialism

item.title: Veterans Affairs Department Cut Thousands of Roles for Doctors and Nurses
item.description: The Trump administration has eliminated health care positions left vacant after a wave of resignations and retirements at the V.A. last year.

item.title: Germany Has Ratcheted Up Defense Spending. In Europe, That Worries Some People.
item.description: Soon the country’s armed forces budget could exceed those of Britain and France combined. In Paris, there are concerns that European “strategic autono...

item.title: Live Updates: U.S. Closes 2 Gulf Embassies as Iran Steps Up Retaliation
item.description: The State Department urged Americans to immediately leave 14 countries, and closed U.S. embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait after they were hit by dr...

item.title: How the Bombing of Iran Is Affecting Lebanon, Kuwait and Other Countries
item.description: The Middle East is facing deaths and destruction as Iran retaliates against a huge American-Israeli military campaign.

item.title: An Emboldened Israel Is Seizing Opportunities to Remake Region
item.description: With the war against Iran underway, and the U.S. military as a powerful ally, the Israeli government is seizing its chance to move against other adver...

item.title: Germany Has Ratcheted Up Defense Spending. In Europe, That Worries Some People.
item.description: Soon the country’s armed forces budget could exceed those of Britain and France combined. In Paris, there are concerns that European “strategic autono...
